channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: White House denies reports that Elon Musk is being pushed out
item.description: A report from Politico claims Trump has told his inner circle that Musk will soon take his leave from the White House.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Shafer, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's changes to the federal government aren't yet a clear political winner or loser: AP-NORC poll
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump's dramatic changes to the federal government haven't emerged as an obvious political winner or loser, according to a new poll that indicates some Americans may be giving him the benefit of the doubt for now on his Department of Government Efficiency.
item.dc:creator: AMELIA THOMSON-DEVEAUX and JONATHAN J. COOPER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 5 takeaways from Trump's major tariff announcement
item.description: “Our country and its taxpayers have been ripped off for more than 50 years, but it’s not going to happen anymore,” Trump said.
item.dc:creator: Tobias Burns, NewsNation

item.title: GOP defectors help Senate advance resolution to cancel Trump tariffs despite White House veto warning
item.description: The Senate voted on a resolution to undo Trump’s tariffs against Canada on Wednesday, and some Republicans went on record opposing the president.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Answering your questions about President Trump's vast new tariffs
item.description: After weeks of anticipation and speculation, President Donald Trump followed through on his reciprocal tariff threats by declaring on Wednesday a 10% baseline tax on imports from all countries and higher tariff rates on dozens of nations that run trade surpluses with the United States.
item.dc:creator: Christopher Rugaber And Paul Wiseman, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Johnson: 'Working on every possible accommodation' for mothers amid parental proxy voting impasse
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) on Wednesday said he is “working on every possible accommodation” to make it easier for mothers to serve in Congress amid the impasse between GOP leadership and Rep. Anna Paulina Luna (R-Fla.) over parental proxy voting.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate budget blueprint empowers GOP chair to decide if Trump tax cuts add to deficit
item.description: Senate Republicans on Wednesday unveiled a 70-page budget resolution that they say would give Senate Budget Committee Chair Lindsey Graham (R-S.C.), a staunch ally of President Trump, the power to determine whether extending the 2017 Trump tax cuts officially adds to the federal deficit.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Once common, now unusual: Conservative candidate publicly quashes shouts that the other side cheated
item.description: PEWAUKEE, Wis. (AP) — As the first news outlets began calling the Wisconsin Supreme Court election for the liberal candidate Susan Crawford, her opponent called her — to concede.
item.dc:creator: ALI SWENSON and CHRISTINE FERNANDO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate Dems foreshadow more forced votes to block Trump's emergency orders
item.description: Senate Democrats may move to challenge additional Trump emergencies and force floor votes on them, Sen. Tim Kaine suggested at a press conference on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump announces $100M deal with law firm tied to Doug Emhoff, Jan. 6 House panel
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday announced his administration struck a deal with a law firm with ties to former second gentleman Doug Emhoff and the House panel that investigated the Jan. 6 attack on the Capitol as Trump targets major firms for retribution.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Proposed cut in local aid to prevent aquatic invasive species sparks fears of more infested lakes
item.description: For the past decade, Minnesota has distributed about $10 million a year to counties for aquatic invasive species prevention. Gov. Tim Walz’s proposed budget reduced that amount by 50 percent. Some lake advocates worry cutting the funding could potentially lead to more infested waters.
item.dc:creator: Kirsti Marohn, MPR News

item.title: Who is Susan Crawford? Wisconsin Supreme Court winner has fought for union and abortion rights
item.description: Wisconsin Supreme Court winner Susan Crawford has served as a Dane County Circuit Court judge since 2018. Crawford won election that year and again in 2022 to the seat. The county is home to Wisconsin’s liberal capital, Madison.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump unveils 'Liberation Day' tariffs, raising trade tensions
item.description: The tariffs goals include reducing the flow of fentanyl into the U.S. and boosting American-made manufacturing.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: State Sen. Napoleon Harris wins Thornton Township supervisor seat, Jason House victorious in Dolton mayoral race
item.description: Village of Dolton Senior Trustee Jason House won the election for Dolton mayor by a landslide, while State Sen. Napoleon B. Harris III claimed victory to become the Thornton Township Supervisor-elect, unseating the incumbent, self-proclaimed “Super Mayor” Tiffany Henyard.
item.dc:creator: Courtney Spinelli, WGN 9

item.title: California lawmakers reject bills to ban trans athletes' participation in girls sports
item.description: California lawmakers won't change state policies allowing transgender kids and teens to play on sports teams consistent with their gender identities.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIE AUSTIN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Layoffs begin at U.S. health agencies responsible for research, tracking disease and regulating food
item.description: Employees across the massive U.S. Health and Human Services Department began receiving notices of dismissal Tuesday in an overhaul ultimately expected to lay off up to 10,000 people.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Wisconsin voters to choose top education official and consider voter ID amendment
item.description: Wisconsin is electing its top education official, who will guide policies affecting K-12 schools during President Donald Trump’s second term. Tuesday’s vote pits the teachers union-backed incumbent Jill Underly against Republican-supported critic Brittany Kinser.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Election Day: Suburban voters head to the polls
item.description: Several elections will take place in Chicago-area suburbs on Tuesday, including a notable one in Dolton where incumbent Tiffany Henyard will run against Jason House, who won the primary election in February.
item.dc:creator: Winnie Dortch, WGN 9

item.title: Minnesota insurance costs could continue to rise under new Trump tariffs
item.description: Tariffs are taxes on goods imported from other countries, but these new taxes are not just impacting the prices of our T-shirts and televisions. Tariffs may even lead to higher insurance rates here at home.
item.dc:creator: Tom Crann and Gretchen Brown, MPR News

item.title: Federal employees union sues over Trump move to block collective bargaining
item.description: A federal employee union has sued to block a Trump administration order that would strip bargaining rights at more than a dozen departments.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: California Assembly Democrats announce plans to leave X over disinformation, hate speech
item.description: California Assembly Democrats announced plans to leave X over disinformation, lack of content moderation and hate speech.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5
